keyword_lines: MODE 0: Split input to both TX0 and TX1. FRL5 and VRR are supported.
MODE 1: Split input to both TX0 and TX1. TX0 upscales up to FRL5. No VRR supported.
MODE 4: Matrix mode. TX1 downscale. FRL
VRRoom will reboot when switching between Split / Matrix modes
Copy the connected TX0 sink EDID capabilities. No modifications.
Copy the connected TX1 sink EDID capabilities. No modifications.
Standard EDID table to verify cable issues. EDID max caps 4K60 420 300MHz 8b stereo audio.
Custom EDID tables for various situations. The first 10 tables can be replaced over LAN.
COPY TX SINK EDID and MODIFY (AUTOMIX)
Automix EDID tables. Customize capabilities of the connected TX0 / TX1 sink EDID table.
Selected TX SINK will be used for the main video capabilities.
Use DV creator page in CUSTOM mode
In CUSTOM mode the base audio capabilities are copied from TX SINK selected port, and
SAVE ACTIVE EDID TO CUSTOM EDID #:
Use custom HDR for TX0 and TX1 [ignore RX HDR]
Use custom HDR for TX0 and TX1 when input is HDR10 [ignore RX HDR]
Use custom HDR for TX0 and TX1 when input is HLG [ignore RX HDR]
Use custom HDR for TX0 and TX1 when input is HLG and display has no HLG support
Use custom HDR for TX0 and TX1 when input is LLDV
Disable output HDR for TX0 and TX1 when input is HDR10
Disable output HDR for TX0 and TX1 when input is HLG
Disable output LLDV for TX0 and TX1 when input is LLDV
Use custom AVI [for advanced users only]
Use custom VSI [for advanced users only]
Allows to control the TV supported ALLM feature
ON -setting forces ALLM enabled at all times - ignores RX ALLM
OFF -setting forces ALLM disabled at all times - ignores RX ALLM
FOLLOW -setting lets the source control enable/disable
CAPTURED INPUT DOLBY VISION METADATA [from source]:
CONNECTED DOLBY VISION EDID DATABLOCK [sink tx0]:
CONSTRUCT CUSTOM DOLBY VISION DATA BLOCK (for advanced users only):
Generates a 12-byte DV Version 2 Data Block. Select AUTOMIX with CUSTOM STRING -flag to activate.
Standard DV and low latency 12Bit YCbCr 422
VRRoom has three separate CEC zones to support CEC commands and ARC/eARC communication.
MAIN ZONE CEC controls channel switching and eARC/ARC retrieval from the TX0 connected display
eARC TX zone controls the volume and power up/down of the eARC TX connected AVR/Soundbar as well as eARC/ARC transmission
All changes to CEC/eARC/ARC parameters require a VRROOM power cycle. Unplug/replug power
MAIN ZONE CEC must be enabled to support CEC channel switching and eARC/ARC retrieval
eARC/ARC support requires "Audio System", make sure no other AVR devices are connected to the TV
eARC can support lossless bitstream formats, such as MAT atmos, TrueHD, DTS:X, PCM 7.1
ARC can support up to of DD+ Atmos, stereo PCM
AUTO relies on TV CEC commands for switching between eARC/ARC and HDMI. Select the auto mode according to TV capabilities
If the display supports eARC, select Manual eARC. All audio will be routed via TV eARC
If the display supports ARC only, select Manual ARC. All audio will be routed via TV ARC
ARC is max DD+. Consider EDID settings to not exceed the max capability from the HDMI input
Some TVs will incorrectly stream mark 7.1 for all eARC content with more speakers than stereo
MUTE from TV/source remotes can use dedicated CEC mute on/off commands or a toggle command
If the connected device only supports ARC, choose the Always ARC-option, otherwise leave as default
When using Always ARC-option make sure input port AUDIO CAPS don't exceed soundbar/AVR capabilities
Typical ARC soundbar supports maximum DD+ and stereo PCM. Use ARC/BEAM1 or ARC/DD+ AUDIO CAPS
VRRoom issues automated power on/off CEC commands to the AUDIO OUT connected sink.
Request Audio Mode = Instructs the sink to enter System Audio mode and to enable volume control
Request Audio Mode + Routing Change = As above but also issues Routing Change to switch to the VRRoom input
VRRoom has a capability to individually disable CEC from inputs.
Time offset in milliseconds to start sending audio data to the sink. 50msec min
Additional time offset in milliseconds to start sending audio data to the eARC TX.
VRROOM will listen to this port for IP / Telnet commands

status_fields.MODE: MODE 0 - SPLITTER TX0/TX1 FRL5 VRR MODE 1 - SPLITTER TX0/TX1 UPSCALE FRL5 MODE 2 - MATRIX TMDS MODE 3 - MATRIX FRL->TMDS MODE 4 - MATRIX DOWNSCALE MODE 5 - MATRIX RX0:FRL5 + RX1-3:TMDS MODE 0: Split input to both TX0 and TX1. FRL5 and VRR are supported. MODE 1: Split input to both TX0 and TX1. TX0 upscales up to FRL5. No VRR supported. MODE 2: Matrix mode. FRL not supported. MODE 3: Matrix mode. TX1 FRL->TMDS not supported. MODE 4: Matrix mode. TX1 downscale. FRL not supported VRRoom will reboot when switching between Split / Matrix modes PORT SELECTOR: TX0: 0 - INPUT 0 1 - INPUT 1 2 - INPUT 2 3 - INPUT 3 - COPY TX1 - TX1: 0 - INPUT 0 1 - INPUT 1 2 - INPUT 2 3 - INPUT 3 - COPY TX0 - COPY TX0 SINK Copy the connected TX0 sink EDID capabilities. Select AUTOMIX with CUSTOM STRING -flag to activate. DV Interface: Low latency 12Bit YCbCr 422 Low latency 12Bit YCbCr 422 and RGB/YCbCr 444 10/12Bit Standard DV and low latency 12Bit YCbCr 422 Standard DV and low latency 422/444/RGB Primaries: ITU-R BT 709-5 ITU-R BT 2020-2 DCI P3 CUSTOM YUV422 12bit: not supported supported Global Dimming: not supported supported Backlight Ctrl: not supported supported Backlight Minlum: 25 nits 50 nits 75 nits 100 nits Support 10/12b: 10/12b disabled 10b 444/RGB 12b 444/RGB RED Primary X: RED primary Y: GREEN primary X: GREEN primary Y: BLUE primary X: BLUE primary Y: MAX luminance [nits]: Range 100 - 10000 nits MIN luminance [nits]: Range 0 - 1 nits Create Default Send DV OSD SETUP: [press SEND after config] OSD ENABLE FADE SECONDS: [OSD video] FADE SECONDS: [OSD no input] OSD LOCATION [X]: OSD LOCATION [Y]: OSD COLOR [FRONT]: OSD COLOR [BACK]: OSD COLOR [LEVEL]: OSD INFO FLAGS: Source Name Video Field: Video IF: HDR RX/TX Info: HDR IF: Audio Field: Rx Video Info: RX HDR info: Ignore Metadata Change: OSD MASK SETUP: OSD MASK ENABLE OSD MASK [X]: OSD MASK [Y]: OSD MASK1 [X]: OSD MASK1 [Y]: OSD MASK LEVEL: OSD GRAYSCALE: OSD TEXT SETUP: OSD TEXT ENABLE NEVER FADE OSD TEXT [X]: OSD TEXT [Y]: TEXT TO BE DISPLAYED: CEC / eARC / ARC CONTROL: VRRoom has three separate CEC zones to support CEC commands and ARC/eARC communication. MAIN ZONE CEC controls channel switching and eARC/ARC retrieval from the TX0 connected display AUDIO OUT zone controls the volume and power up/down of the AUDIO OUT connected AVR/Soundbar eARC TX zone controls the volume and power up/down of the eARC TX connected AVR/Soundbar as well as eARC/ARC transmission All changes to CEC/eARC/ARC parameters require a VRROOM power cycle. Unplug/replug power MAIN ZONE: MAIN ZONE CEC must be enabled to support CEC channel switching and eARC/ARC retrieval eARC/ARC support requires "Audio System", make sure no other AVR devices are connected to the TV eARC can support lossless bitstream formats, such as MAT atmos, TrueHD, DTS:X, PCM 7.1 ARC can support up to of DD+ Atmos, stereo PCM CEC: Audio System [eARC/ARC] Video Processor [no eARC/ARC] CEC is enabled when the checkbox is selected eARC/ARC: Auto eARC/HDMI Manual eARC Manual HDMI Auto ARC/HDMI Manual ARC AUTO relies on TV CEC commands for switching between eARC/ARC and HDMI. Select the auto mode according to TV capabilities If the display supports eARC, select Manual eARC. All audio will be routed via TV eARC If the display supports ARC only, select Manual ARC. All audio will be routed via TV ARC ARC is max DD+. Consider EDID settings to not exceed the max capability from the HDMI input SPEAKER MAP Automatic Force 5.1 Some TVs will incorrectly stream mark 7.1 for all eARC content with more speakers than stereo It usually results in 5.1 speaker systems to lose the surrounds Use the Force 5.1 -setting to correct the speaker mapping in such systems MUTE MODE MUTE on/off MUTE toggle MUTE from TV/source remotes can use dedicated CEC mute on/off commands or a toggle command Some TVs will cause the toggle command to lose track if pressed many times too quickly eARC TX ZONE: OUT MODE: Always ARC Auto eARC/ARC [default] If the connected device only supports ARC, choose the Always ARC-option, otherwise leave as default When using Always ARC-option make sure input port AUDIO CAPS don't exceed soundbar/AVR capabilities Typical ARC soundbar supports maximum DD+ and stereo PCM. Use ARC/BEAM1 or ARC/DD+ AUDIO CAPS AUDIO OUT ZONE: POWER ON: Disabled Request Audio Mode Request Audio Mode + Routing Change POWER OFF: Disabled Automatic VRRoom issues automated power on/off CEC commands to the AUDIO OUT connected sink. Request Audio Mode = Instructs the sink to enter System Audio mode and to enable volume control Request Audio Mode + Routing Change = As above but also issues Routing Change to switch to the VRRoom input INPUT PORT CEC CONTROL: VRRoom has a capability to individually disable CEC from inputs. When disabled, no CEC commands will be allowed from the respective input. IN0 IN1 IN2 IN3 Checked means enabled. Default is checked. RS232 COMMANDS: RS232 BAUD RATE: 9600 19200 57600 115200 Send Ascii Send Binary MACRO CONTROL: ENABLE SEND ON EVERY SYNC HDR10 MODE: Automatic (default) Ignore Metadata Semi-Automatic Custom SYNC DELAY (secs): CONTENT/CALIBRATION PICTURE MODE OSD NAME CUSTOM HEX SDR-BT709 (HDTV) DO NOTHING HDR10 HLG USER 1 USER 2 USER 3 USER 4 USER 5 (2015+) USER 6 (2015+) FILM NATURAL ANIMATION THX STAGE (2014 only) GAMMA NORMAL GAMMA A GAMMA B GAMMA C GAMMA CUSTOM 1 GAMMA CUSTOM 2 GAMMA CUSTOM 3 GAMMA D GAMMA FILM 1 GAMMA FILM 2 FRAME ADAPT HDR CUSTOM SDR-TV SDR-BT709 (Film) SDR-Film SDR-BT2020 SDR-BT2020 3D 3D x.v.Color x.v.Color HLG-BT709 HLG-BT709 HLG-BT2020 HLG-BT2020 HDR10 HDR10-DC4K HDR10-Opt HDR10-DC1K HDR10-Rec709 HDR10-BT709 LLDV LLDV ALWAYS EXECUTE CUSTOM HEX BEFORE AFTER THE SELECTED PICTURE MODE WITH A DELAY OF 1 sec 3 sec 5 sec 7 sec 10 sec 15 sec 20 sec 25 sec CUSTOM HDR10 PROFILES: IF HDR10 Max Brightness Min Brightness MaxCLL BELOW OR EQUALS TO THEN IF HDR10 Max Brightness Min Brightness MaxCLL BELOW OR EQUALS TO THEN IF HDR10 Max Brightness Min Brightness MaxCLL BELOW OR EQUALS TO THEN IF HDR10 Max Brightness Min Brightness MaxCLL BELOW OR EQUALS TO THEN IF HDR10 Max Brightness Min Brightness MaxCLL BELOW OR EQUALS TO THEN IF HDR10 Max Brightness Min Brightness MaxCLL ABOVE THEN ELSE VRROOM SETUP: AUTOSWITCH INPUTS: OFF ON POWER HDCP VERSION: 1.4 Auto HTPC MODE RX0: ON OFF HTPC MODE RX1: ON OFF HTPC MODE RX2: ON OFF HTPC MODE RX3: ON OFF AUDIO UNMUTE: SEND Time offset in milliseconds to start sending audio data to the sink. 50msec min eARC UNMUTE: SEND Additional time offset in milliseconds to start sending audio data to the eARC TX. OLED DISPLAY: ON OFF OLED FADE: SEND OLED PAGE: INFO IP RX INFO HDR 1 HDR 2 IF INFO REBOOT TIMER(hours): SEND AUDIO OUT FORMAT: 720p60 1080p60 MUTE TX0 AUDIO: ON OFF MUTE TX1 AUDIO: ON OFF IR: ON OFF IR CODE SET: SET 1 SET 2 SET 3 TX1 +5V FORCE: ON OFF RELAY: ON OFF ANALOG L/R AUDIO CONTROL: VOLUME [-100 to +10dB] BASS [-10 to +10dB] TREBLE [-10 to +10dB] IP CONFIG: Reboot required for any changes in DHCP / STATIC IP DHCP: ON OFF mDNS: ON OFF Current IP Configuration: IP ADDR: IP MASK: IP GW: MAC: Configure Static IP: This address is used when DHCP is off IP ADDR: IP MASK: IP GW: SET IP Configure TCP port: [requires reboot to apply] VRROOM will listen to this port for IP / Telnet commands IP INTERRUPTS: ON OFF IP PORT: SET PORT VRROOM CONTROL: REBOOT UNIT REBOOT EXPORT CONFIG EXPORT IMPORT CONFIG ISSUE HOTPLUG SEND RESET TO FACTORY DEFAULTS: CLEAR ALL SETTINGS TO FACTORY DEFAULTS: EDID FACTORY SETTINGS: RESET FW UPDATE: Refresh

MODE 0: Split input to both TX0 and TX1. FRL5 and VRR are supported.
MODE 1: Split input to both TX0 and TX1. TX0 upscales up to FRL5. No VRR supported.
MODE 4: Matrix mode. TX1 downscale. FRL
VRRoom will reboot when switching between Split / Matrix modes
Copy the connected TX0 sink EDID capabilities. No modifications.

COPY TX SINK EDID and MODIFY (AUTOMIX)

Use DV creator page in CUSTOM mode

SAVE ACTIVE EDID TO CUSTOM EDID #:

Use custom HDR for TX0 and TX1 when input is HDR10 [ignore RX HDR]
Use custom HDR for TX0 and TX1 when input is HLG [ignore RX HDR]
Use custom HDR for TX0 and TX1 when input is HLG and display has no HLG support

Allows to control the TV supported ALLM feature
ON -setting forces ALLM enabled at all times - ignores RX ALLM
OFF -setting forces ALLM disabled at all times - ignores RX ALLM
FOLLOW -setting lets the source control enable/disable
CAPTURED INPUT DOLBY VISION METADATA [from source]:
CONNECTED DOLBY VISION EDID DATABLOCK [sink tx0]:
CONSTRUCT CUSTOM DOLBY VISION DATA BLOCK (for advanced users only):
Generates a 12-byte DV Version 2 Data Block. Select AUTOMIX with CUSTOM STRING -flag to activate.
Standard DV and low latency 12Bit YCbCr 422
VRRoom has three separate CEC zones to support CEC commands and ARC/eARC communication.
MAIN ZONE CEC controls channel switching and eARC/ARC retrieval from the TX0 connected display
eARC TX zone controls the volume and power up/down of the eARC TX connected AVR/Soundbar as well as eARC/ARC transmission
All changes to CEC/eARC/ARC parameters require a VRROOM power cycle. Unplug/replug power
MAIN ZONE CEC must be enabled to support CEC channel switching and eARC/ARC retrieval
eARC/ARC support requires "Audio System", make sure no other AVR devices are connected to the TV
eARC can support lossless bitstream formats, such as MAT atmos, TrueHD, DTS:X, PCM 7.1
ARC can support up to of DD+ Atmos, stereo PCM
AUTO relies on TV CEC commands for switching between eARC/ARC and HDMI. Select the auto mode according to TV capabilities
If the display supports eARC, select Manual eARC. All audio will be routed via TV eARC
If the display supports ARC only, select Manual ARC. All audio will be routed via TV ARC
ARC is max DD+. Consider EDID settings to not exceed the max capability from the HDMI input
Some TVs will incorrectly stream mark 7.1 for all eARC content with more speakers than stereo
MUTE from TV/source remotes can use dedicated CEC mute on/off commands or a toggle command
If the connected device only supports ARC, choose the Always ARC-option, otherwise leave as default
When using Always ARC-option make sure input port AUDIO CAPS don't exceed soundbar/AVR capabilities
Typical ARC soundbar supports maximum DD+ and stereo PCM. Use ARC/BEAM1 or ARC/DD+ AUDIO CAPS
VRRoom issues automated power on/off CEC commands to the AUDIO OUT connected sink.
Request Audio Mode = Instructs the sink to enter System Audio mode and to enable volume control
Request Audio Mode + Routing Change = As above but also issues Routing Change to switch to the VRRoom input
VRRoom has a capability to individually disable CEC from inputs.
Time offset in milliseconds to start sending audio data to the sink. 50msec min
Additional time offset in milliseconds to start sending audio data to the eARC TX.
VRROOM will listen to this port for IP / Telnet commands

FRL5 and VRR are supported. MODE 1: Split input to both TX0 and TX1. TX0 upscales up to FRL5. No VRR supported. MODE 2: Matrix mode. FRL not supported. MODE 3: Matrix mode. TX1 FRL->TMDS not supported. MODE 4: Matrix mode. TX1 downscale. FRL not supported VRRoom will reboot when switching between Split / Matrix modes PORT SELECTOR: TX0: 0 - INPUT 0 1 - INPUT 1 2 - INPUT 2 3 - INPUT 3 - COPY TX1 - TX1: 0 - INPUT 0 1 - INPUT 1 2 - INPUT 2 3 - INPUT 3 - COPY TX0 - COPY TX0 SINK Copy the connected TX0 sink EDID capabilities. INPUT 2 3 - INPUT 3 - ALL - UHD ALLIANCE FILM MAKER MODE: Enable Film Maker Mode OTHER CAPTURED METADATA: SPD INFOFRAME: AUD INFOFRAME: HVS INFOFRAME: VSI INFOFRAME: HFV INFOFRAME: CAPTURED INPUT DOLBY VISION METADATA [from source]: DV Status: DV interface: Backlight metadata: Backlight Max Luminance: Auxiliary runmode: Auxiliary version: Auxiliary debug: CONNECTED DOLBY VISION EDID DATABLOCK [sink tx0]: DV Version: DV DM Version: DV Interface: 2160p60: YUV422 12bit: Global Dimming: Backlight Control: RED Primary: GREEN Primary: BLUE Primary: MAX/MIN Luminance: CONSTRUCT CUSTOM DOLBY VISION DATA BLOCK (for advanced users only): Generates a 12-byte DV Version 2 Data Block. Select AUTOMIX with CUSTOM STRING -flag to activate. DV Interface: Low latency 12Bit YCbCr 422 Low latency 12Bit YCbCr 422 and RGB/YCbCr 444 10/12Bit Standard DV and low latency 12Bit YCbCr 422 Standard DV and low latency 422/444/RGB Primaries: ITU-R BT 709-5 ITU-R BT 2020-2 DCI P3 CUSTOM YUV422 12bit: not supported supported Global Dimming: not supported supported Backlight Ctrl: not supported supported Backlight Minlum: 25 nits 50 nits 75 nits 100 nits Support 10/12b: 10/12b disabled 10b 444/RGB 12b 444/RGB RED Primary X: RED primary Y: GREEN primary X: GREEN primary Y: BLUE primary X: BLUE primary Y: MAX luminance [nits]: Range 100 - 10000 nits MIN luminance [nits]: Range 0 - 1 nits Create Default Send DV OSD SETUP: [press SEND after config] OSD ENABLE FADE SECONDS: [OSD video] FADE SECONDS: [OSD no input] OSD LOCATION [X]: OSD LOCATION [Y]: OSD COLOR [FRONT]: OSD COLOR [BACK]: OSD COLOR [LEVEL]: OSD INFO FLAGS: Source Name Video Field: Video IF: HDR RX/TX Info: HDR IF: Audio Field: Rx Video Info: RX HDR info: Ignore Metadata Change: OSD MASK SETUP: OSD MASK ENABLE OSD MASK [X]: OSD MASK [Y]: OSD MASK1 [X]: OSD MASK1 [Y]: OSD MASK LEVEL: OSD GRAYSCALE: OSD TEXT SETUP: OSD TEXT ENABLE NEVER FADE OSD TEXT [X]: OSD TEXT [Y]: TEXT TO BE DISPLAYED: CEC / eARC / ARC CONTROL: VRRoom has three separate CEC zones to support CEC commands and ARC/eARC communication. MAIN ZONE CEC controls channel switching and eARC/ARC retrieval from the TX0 connected display AUDIO OUT zone controls the volume and power up/down of the AUDIO OUT connected AVR/Soundbar eARC TX zone controls the volume and power up/down of the eARC TX connected AVR/Soundbar as well as eARC/ARC transmission All changes to CEC/eARC/ARC parameters require a VRROOM power cycle. Unplug/replug power MAIN ZONE: MAIN ZONE CEC must be enabled to support CEC channel switching and eARC/ARC retrieval eARC/ARC support requires "Audio System", make sure no other AVR devices are connected to the TV eARC can support lossless bitstream formats, such as MAT atmos, TrueHD, DTS:X, PCM 7.1 ARC can support up to of DD+ Atmos, stereo PCM CEC: Audio System [eARC/ARC] Video Processor [no eARC/ARC] CEC is enabled when the checkbox is selected eARC/ARC: Auto eARC/HDMI Manual eARC Manual HDMI Auto ARC/HDMI Manual ARC AUTO relies on TV CEC commands for switching between eARC/ARC and HDMI. Select the auto mode according to TV capabilities If the display supports eARC, select Manual eARC. All audio will be routed via TV eARC If the display supports ARC only, select Manual ARC. All audio will be routed via TV ARC ARC is max DD+. Consider EDID settings to not exceed the max capability from the HDMI input SPEAKER MAP Automatic Force 5.1 Some TVs will incorrectly stream mark 7.1 for all eARC content with more speakers than stereo It usually results in 5.1 speaker systems to lose the surrounds Use the Force 5.1 -setting to correct the speaker mapping in such systems MUTE MODE MUTE on/off MUTE toggle MUTE from TV/source remotes can use dedicated CEC mute on/off commands or a toggle command Some TVs will cause the toggle command to lose track if pressed many times too quickly eARC TX ZONE: OUT MODE: Always ARC Auto eARC/ARC [default] If the connected device only supports ARC, choose the Always ARC-option, otherwise leave as default When using Always ARC-option make sure input port AUDIO CAPS don't exceed soundbar/AVR capabilities Typical ARC soundbar supports maximum DD+ and stereo PCM. Use ARC/BEAM1 or ARC/DD+ AUDIO CAPS AUDIO OUT ZONE: POWER ON: Disabled Request Audio Mode Request Audio Mode + Routing Change POWER OFF: Disabled Automatic VRRoom issues automated power on/off CEC commands to the AUDIO OUT connected sink. Request Audio Mode = Instructs the sink to enter System Audio mode and to enable volume control Request Audio Mode + Routing Change = As above but also issues Routing Change to switch to the VRRoom input INPUT PORT CEC CONTROL: VRRoom has a capability to individually disable CEC from inputs. When disabled, no CEC commands will be allowed from the respective input. IN0 IN1 IN2 IN3 Checked means enabled. Default is checked. RS232 COMMANDS: RS232 BAUD RATE: 9600 19200 57600 115200 Send Ascii Send Binary MACRO CONTROL: ENABLE SEND ON EVERY SYNC HDR10 MODE: Automatic (default) Ignore Metadata Semi-Automatic Custom SYNC DELAY (secs): CONTENT/CALIBRATION PICTURE MODE OSD NAME CUSTOM HEX SDR-BT709 (HDTV) DO NOTHING HDR10 HLG USER 1 USER 2 USER 3 USER 4 USER 5 (2015+) USER 6 (2015+) FILM NATURAL ANIMATION THX STAGE (2014 only) GAMMA NORMAL GAMMA A GAMMA B GAMMA C GAMMA CUSTOM 1 GAMMA CUSTOM 2 GAMMA CUSTOM 3 GAMMA D GAMMA FILM 1 GAMMA FILM 2 FRAME ADAPT HDR CUSTOM SDR-TV SDR-BT709 (Film) SDR-Film SDR-BT2020 SDR-BT2020 3D 3D x.v.Color x.v.Color HLG-BT709 HLG-BT709 HLG-BT2020 HLG-BT2020 HDR10 HDR10-DC4K HDR10-Opt HDR10-DC1K HDR10-Rec709 HDR10-BT709 LLDV LLDV ALWAYS EXECUTE CUSTOM HEX BEFORE AFTER THE SELECTED PICTURE MODE WITH A DELAY OF 1 sec 3 sec 5 sec 7 sec 10 sec 15 sec 20 sec 25 sec CUSTOM HDR10 PROFILES: IF HDR10 Max Brightness Min Brightness MaxCLL BELOW OR EQUALS TO THEN IF HDR10 Max Brightness Min Brightness MaxCLL BELOW OR EQUALS TO THEN IF HDR10 Max Brightness Min Brightness MaxCLL BELOW OR EQUALS TO THEN IF HDR10 Max Brightness Min Brightness MaxCLL BELOW OR EQUALS TO THEN IF HDR10 Max Brightness Min Brightness MaxCLL BELOW OR EQUALS TO THEN IF HDR10 Max Brightness Min Brightness MaxCLL ABOVE THEN ELSE VRROOM SETUP: AUTOSWITCH INPUTS: OFF ON POWER HDCP VERSION: 1.4 Auto HTPC MODE RX0: ON OFF HTPC MODE RX1: ON OFF HTPC MODE RX2: ON OFF HTPC MODE RX3: ON OFF AUDIO UNMUTE: SEND Time offset in milliseconds to start sending audio data to the sink. 50msec min eARC UNMUTE: SEND Additional time offset in milliseconds to start sending audio data to the eARC TX. OLED DISPLAY: ON OFF OLED FADE: SEND OLED PAGE: INFO IP RX INFO HDR 1 HDR 2 IF INFO REBOOT TIMER(hours): SEND AUDIO OUT FORMAT: 720p60 1080p60 MUTE TX0 AUDIO: ON OFF MUTE TX1 AUDIO: ON OFF IR: ON OFF IR CODE SET: SET 1 SET 2 SET 3 TX1 +5V FORCE: ON OFF RELAY: ON OFF ANALOG L/R AUDIO CONTROL: VOLUME [-100 to +10dB] BASS [-10 to +10dB] TREBLE [-10 to +10dB] IP CONFIG: Reboot required for any changes in DHCP / STATIC IP DHCP: ON OFF mDNS: ON OFF Current IP Configuration: IP ADDR: IP MASK: IP GW: MAC: Configure Static IP: This address is used when DHCP is off IP ADDR: IP MASK: IP GW: SET IP Configure TCP port: [requires reboot to apply] VRROOM will listen to this port for IP / Telnet commands IP INTERRUPTS: ON OFF IP PORT: SET PORT VRROOM CONTROL: REBOOT UNIT REBOOT EXPORT CONFIG EXPORT IMPORT CONFIG ISSUE HOTPLUG SEND RESET TO FACTORY DEFAULTS: CLEAR ALL SETTINGS TO FACTORY DEFAULTS: EDID FACTORY SETTINGS: RESET FW UPDATE: Refresh
